indicator_text: Unique identifier of a quarterly LDA lobbying report (the unit of disclosure)
scoring_rule: N/A (data field, not a scored indicator). Primary key for the Reports table; foreign-keyed by Issues, Texts, and bill_client_networks.
source_quote: report_uuid: Unique identifier of the report (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — reports() endpoint docstring); "based on the universe of lobbying reports filed under the Lobbying Disclosure Act of 1995" (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:9-10)
notes: Federal LDA. State-level analog: each state defines its own report identifier scheme; harmonization layer needed. Cross-ref items_Kim2018:lobbying_report.

indicator_text: Unique identifier of the lobbying client (the entity hiring lobbyists, a.k.a. "interest group")
scoring_rule: N/A. Standardized identifier produced by LobbyView's name-disambiguation pipeline (NLP + collaborative filtering, per Kim 2018).
source_quote: client_uuid: Unique identifier of the client (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — clients() endpoint); "identify political actors and their lobbying activities based on standardized firm- and industry-level identifiers to facilitate systemic research on lobbying and merging with external datasets" (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:12-14)
notes: LobbyView-added enrichment. State-level analog: each state has registrant/client tables; cross-state and cross-time disambiguation is non-trivial. Cross-ref items_Kim2018:firm.

indicator_text: Name of the lobbying client (the registrant's principal / hiring entity)
scoring_rule: N/A. Searchable via partial match (ilike) in API.
source_quote: client_name: Name of the client - using partial match with ilike operator (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — clients() endpoint)
notes: LDA-direct field (LDA Form LD-1/LD-2 client name). Always disclosed at federal level. State-level: most states require this.

indicator_text: Primary NAICS code for the client (industry classification)
scoring_rule: N/A. NAICS code range queryable via min_naics/max_naics.
source_quote: min_naics, max_naics: NAICS code range filters (North American Industry Classification System) (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — clients() endpoint); "sorted by industry as identified by NAICS2" (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:307-308)
notes: LobbyView-added enrichment (industry-classification merge — typically Compustat/Crunchbase/manual). Cross-ref items_Kim2018:industry_naics.

indicator_text: Free-text description(s) of the NAICS code(s) for the client (array)
scoring_rule: N/A. Array — clients may have multiple NAICS classifications.
source_quote: naics_description: Descriptions of the NAICS code - using exact match (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — clients() endpoint)
notes: LobbyView-added enrichment. Companion to primary_naics.

indicator_text: Unique identifier of the registrant (the lobbying firm filing the report on the client's behalf, OR the client itself if self-filing)
scoring_rule: N/A. Distinct from client_uuid: the registrant is the filer (lobbying firm or in-house), the client is the represented entity.
source_quote: registrant_uuid: Unique identifier of the registrant (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — reports() endpoint)
notes: LDA-direct concept (registrant = LDA Form LD-1 §3 filer). State-level: state lobbying registers usually distinguish registrant vs client; some don't.

indicator_text: Name of the registrant (lobbying firm or self-filing organization)
scoring_rule: N/A. Searchable via partial match (ilike) in API.
source_quote: registrant_name: Name of the registrant - using partial match with ilike operator (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — reports() endpoint)
notes: LDA-direct field. State-level: usually disclosed.

indicator_text: Calendar year of the lobbying report
scoring_rule: N/A. Used as filter; min_report_year/max_report_year for ranges.
source_quote: report_year: Year of the report (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — reports() endpoint); "consider a range of candidate Congresses extending backwards from the Congress containing the year that the lobbying report was filed" (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:63-64)
notes: LDA-direct field. Cross-ref items_Kim2018:report_filing_year.

indicator_text: Quarter period of the report (Q1-Q4 string)
scoring_rule: N/A. Returned as string. Federal LDA reports are filed quarterly post-2007 (HLOGA).
source_quote: report_quarter_code: Quarter period of the report (returns quarter as string) (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — reports() endpoint)
notes: LDA-direct field; quarterly cadence is HLOGA-2007 mandate. State-level: cadence varies (annual / semi-annual / quarterly / monthly-when-in-session).

indicator_text: Lobbying firm income (per LDA reporting rule) OR lobbying expense for in-house filers; the dollar figure for the report period
scoring_rule: N/A. Range filtering supported (min_amount, max_amount).
source_quote: min_amount, max_amount: Lobbying firm income or lobbying expense (in-house) (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — reports() endpoint)
notes: LDA-direct field. Federally rounded to nearest $10,000 (LDA reporting rule). State-level: itemization granularity varies enormously.

indicator_text: Boolean flag — the registrant filed an "activity report" with no lobbying activity in the period
scoring_rule: N/A. Used to distinguish active reports from "zero" filings (still required under LDA).
source_quote: is_no_activity: Quarterly activity indicator (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — reports() endpoint)
notes: LDA-direct field. State-level analog: zero-activity reporting requirement varies.

indicator_text: Boolean flag — the registrant is the client (in-house lobbying), not an external lobbying firm
scoring_rule: N/A. In-house = self-filer = registrant_uuid corresponds to client_uuid.
source_quote: is_client_self_filer: An organization employing its own in-house lobbyist(s) (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — reports() endpoint)
notes: LDA-direct concept. State-level: in-house vs contract distinction usually preserved.

indicator_text: Boolean flag — this report amends a previously filed report
scoring_rule: N/A. Distinguishes original vs. amended filings.
source_quote: is_amendment: Amendment of previous report (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — reports() endpoint)
notes: LDA-direct field. State-level: amendments are commonly disclosed but not always flagged.

indicator_text: Ordinal position of an issue within a report (1, 2, 3, …)
scoring_rule: N/A. Compound key with report_uuid identifies a specific issue line.
source_quote: issue_ordi: The ordinal number (position) of the issue within the report (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — issues() endpoint)
notes: LDA-direct: Form LD-2 lists multiple issue codes per report. State-level: per-issue itemization varies.

indicator_text: General Issue Area Code (LDA Form LD-2 Section 15) — standardized two-letter taxonomy code (e.g., 'TRD' = Trade)
scoring_rule: N/A. Filterable; ~80 standardized codes in the LDA taxonomy.
source_quote: issue_code: General Issue Area Code (Section 15) (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — issues() endpoint); "trade = LobbyingReport.query.filter(LobbyingReport.issues.any(LobbyingIssue.code.in_(['TRADE (DOMESTIC/FOREIGN)']))) ... trade.count() 52418" (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:245-251)
notes: LDA-direct (Section 15 standardized codes). State-level: most states use free-text issue descriptions, not a fixed code list. Cross-ref items_Kim2018:lobbying_issue_code.

indicator_text: Houses of Congress and Federal agencies contacted for this issue (LDA Form LD-2 Section 17) — array
scoring_rule: N/A. Array — multiple government entities can be listed per issue.
source_quote: gov_entity: House(s) of Congress and Federal agencies (Section 17) (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — issues() endpoint)
notes: LDA-direct (Section 17). State-level: some states require disclosure of which agency/branch/legislator was contacted; many do not.

indicator_text: Specific Lobbying Issues free-text narrative (LDA Form LD-2 Section 16) — describes what the lobbying activity addressed
scoring_rule: N/A. Full-text searchable via ilike. Indexed in Whoosh in the 2018 implementation; Elasticsearch in production.
source_quote: issue_text: Specific lobbying issues (Section 16) - using partial match with ilike operator (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — texts() endpoint); "the text of each lobbying report specific issue ... duplicated in a separate full-text index database" (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:253-256); "interest group activities are documented in Section 16 under 'Specific Lobbying Issues'" (papers/text/Kim_2025__ai_bill_positions_lobbying.txt:395-396)
notes: LDA-direct (Section 16). The load-bearing field for downstream LLM/GNN annotation in Kim 2025. State-level: free-text issue narratives are common but format varies. Cross-ref items_Kim2018:lobbying_specific_issue.

indicator_text: Unique identifier of a legislator (Bioguide ID)
section_or_category: Legislators table; Bills table FK; Networks table FK
scoring_rule: N/A. Bioguide ID is the canonical Congress.gov member identifier.
source_quote: legislator_id: Unique identifier of the legislator from LobbyView (Bioguide ID) (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — legislators() endpoint)
notes: External-data field (Congress.gov / Bioguide). State-level: each state has its own legislator-ID system; some have OpenStates IDs.

indicator_text: GovTrack identifier for the legislator
scoring_rule: N/A. Cross-reference for joining to GovTrack-sourced datasets.
source_quote: legislator_govtrack_id: Unique identifier of the legislator from GovTrack (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — legislators() endpoint)
notes: External-data field. State-level: GovTrack covers federal only; OpenStates is the state analog.

indicator_text: Legislator's first name
scoring_rule: N/A. Partial match (ilike) supported.
source_quote: legislator_first_name: First name of the legislator - using partial match with ilike operator (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — legislators() endpoint)
notes: External-data field.

indicator_text: Legislator's last name
scoring_rule: N/A. Partial match (ilike) supported.
source_quote: legislator_last_name: Last name of the legislator - using partial match with ilike operator (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — legislators() endpoint)
notes: External-data field.

indicator_text: Legislator's full name (First Middle Last)
scoring_rule: N/A. Partial match (ilike) supported.
source_quote: legislator_full_name: Full name of the legislator (First Middle Last) - using partial match (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — legislators() endpoint)
notes: External-data field.

indicator_text: Legislator's gender
scoring_rule: N/A. Used as a node feature in Kim 2025's GNN.
source_quote: legislator_gender: Gender of the legislator (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — legislators() endpoint); "Likewise, lobbyists are characterized by features including ethnicity, gender, and past party affiliation for former Congress members" (papers/text/Kim_2025__ai_bill_positions_lobbying.txt:538-540)
notes: External-data field.

indicator_text: Legislator's birthday (YYYY-MM-DD)
scoring_rule: N/A. Range filterable (min_birthday, max_birthday).
source_quote: birthday: Exact birthday of the legislator (YYYY-MM-DD) (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — legislators() endpoint)
notes: External-data field.

indicator_text: Session of Congress to which a bill belongs (e.g., 110, 117)
scoring_rule: N/A. Component of bill primary key.
source_quote: congress_number: Session of Congress (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — bills() endpoint); "the id is a string of the form 110 HR7311, where 110 is the number of the congress to which this bill belongs" (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:201-203)
notes: External-data field (Congress.gov). State-level analog: state legislative session number (varies — biennial/annual/special). Cross-ref items_Kim2018:bill.

indicator_text: Chamber of origin (House / Senate / joint)
scoring_rule: N/A. Component of bill identification.
source_quote: bill_chamber: Chamber of the legislative branch (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — bills() endpoint)
notes: External-data field. State-level: most states are bicameral (NE is unicameral); analog applies.

indicator_text: Bill type (e.g., HR, S, HRES, SRES, HJRES, SJRES, HCONRES, SCONRES)
scoring_rule: N/A. Distinguishes bills from resolutions, joint resolutions, etc.
source_quote: bill_resolution_type: Bill type (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — bills() endpoint)
notes: External-data field. State-level: bill-type taxonomies are state-specific (HB/SB/HJR/SR/etc.).

indicator_text: Numeric portion of the bill identifier (e.g., 7311 for HR 7311)
scoring_rule: N/A. Component of bill primary key.
source_quote: bill_number: Bill number (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — bills() endpoint)
notes: External-data field.

indicator_text: Date a bill was introduced to Congress
scoring_rule: N/A. Range filterable (min_introduced_date, max_introduced_date).
source_quote: min_introduced_date, max_introduced_date: Date of introduction to Congress (YYYY-MM-DD) (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — bills() endpoint); "we can get the date that the bill was introduced with b.introduced" (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:206)
notes: External-data field. Cross-ref items_Kim2018:bill_introduced_date.

indicator_text: Date of most recent status change for a bill
scoring_rule: N/A. Range filterable (min_updated_date, max_updated_date).
source_quote: min_updated_date, max_updated_date: Date of most recent status change (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — bills() endpoint)
notes: External-data field.

indicator_text: Status of the bill in the legislative process (e.g., introduced, passed, enacted)
scoring_rule: N/A. Partial match (ilike) supported.
source_quote: bill_state: Bill status - using partial match with ilike operator (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — bills() endpoint); features include "Final State" with values "ENACTED:SIGNED, FAIL, PASSED:BILL,..." (papers/text/Kim_2025__ai_bill_positions_lobbying.txt:2052,2066)
notes: External-data field. Used as Bill node feature in Kim 2025 GNN.

indicator_text: URL to the bill record (typically Congress.gov)
scoring_rule: N/A. Permalink for human-readable bill record.
source_quote: bill_url (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — bills() endpoint, listed in BillResponse fields)
notes: External-data field.

indicator_text: Inferred edge between a lobbying report (i.e., its client) and one or more bills the report discusses — produced by the bill-detection pipeline
scoring_rule: N/A. Compound key: (congress_number, bill_chamber, bill_resolution_type, bill_number, report_uuid, issue_ordi, client_uuid).
source_quote: Bill-Client Network: congress_number, bill_chamber, bill_resolution_type, bill_number, report_uuid, issue_ordi, client_uuid (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — bill_client_networks fields); "LobbyView bridges two distinct observable political behaviors with regard to congressional bills: (1) sponsorship by politicians, and (2) reported lobbying by interest groups" (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:11-12)
notes: LobbyView-added enrichment (the headline contribution of Kim 2018). 5-step pipeline: regex bill numbers + cosine-similarity Congress disambiguation + propagation + title matching + range expansion. Private endpoint — contact lobbydata@gmail.com. Cross-ref items_Kim2018:bill_lobbying_link.

indicator_text: Count of bills sponsored by a legislator that a given client lobbied on, in a given year
scoring_rule: N/A. Range filterable (min_bills_sponsored, max_bills_sponsored). Aggregate of the bill-client edge crossed with bill sponsorship.
source_quote: n_bills_sponsored: Number of bills sponsored by the legislator (LobbyViewPythonPackage README — networks() endpoint)
notes: LobbyView-added enrichment (network-level aggregation across the bill-client and legislator-bill edges).

indicator_text: Interest group's position on a specific bill — Support / Oppose / Amend / Monitor (4-class) or Support / Oppose / Engage (3-class collapsed)
scoring_rule: N/A. LLM+GNN-derived; not in core LobbyView schema as of 2018, added in Kim 2025.
source_quote: capturing a range of bill positions—Support, Oppose, and Engage (i.e., Amend and Monitor)—to offer a more detailed view of lobbying behavior (papers/text/Kim_2025__ai_bill_positions_lobbying.txt:278-280); "We publicly release the bill position dataset and lobbying position scores (LPscores)" (papers/text/Kim_2025__ai_bill_positions_lobbying.txt:293-294)
notes: Kim 2025-added enrichment over LobbyView. Not currently exposed via the public API. State-level analog: virtually no state requires lobbyists to disclose their position (support/oppose); Wisconsin is the notable exception.

indicator_text: Lobbyist's prior covered government employment (LDA Form LD-2 Section 18) — used in Kim 2025 to derive lobbyist-legislator "previously worked together" edges
section_or_category: LDA Section 18 (referenced in Kim 2025 graph schema)
scoring_rule: N/A. Used as edge-construction input in the Kim 2025 GNN; not currently a first-class field in the public LobbyView API.
source_quote: We gather this information from 'Covered Official Position' reported under Section 18 of lobbying reports (papers/text/Kim_2025__ai_bill_positions_lobbying.txt:504-505); "Additionally, a connection between a lobbyist and a legislator is formed if they have previously worked together" (papers/text/Kim_2025__ai_bill_positions_lobbying.txt:460-461)
notes: LDA-direct field (Section 18). Foundational for revolving-door analysis. State-level: revolving-door disclosure varies dramatically — many states require nothing; some require multi-year cooling-off declarations.

indicator_text: Unique identifier of an individual lobbyist (named on a report)
section_or_category: Implicit in Kim 2025 graph schema; not a first-class endpoint in current API
scoring_rule: N/A. Lobbyist nodes are central in Kim 2025 (25,043 lobbyists in the GNN); not exposed as a top-level public-API endpoint as of the 2024 README.
source_quote: Lobbyist 25043 (papers/text/Kim_2025__ai_bill_positions_lobbying.txt:2108-2110); "lobbyists are characterized by features including ethnicity, gender, and past party affiliation for former Congress members" (papers/text/Kim_2025__ai_bill_positions_lobbying.txt:538-540)
notes: LDA-direct field (Form LD-2 lists individual lobbyists per report). LobbyView's name-disambiguation produces a stable identifier for the GNN/network use cases. State-level: most states require named lobbyists per registration.

indicator_text: Lobbyist demographic features used in Kim 2025 GNN (ethnicity, gender, party affiliation)
scoring_rule: N/A. Inferred (e.g., name → ethnicity); not a raw LDA field.
source_quote: Likewise, lobbyists are characterized by features including ethnicity, gender, and past party affiliation for former Congress members (papers/text/Kim_2025__ai_bill_positions_lobbying.txt:538-540); Table 6 row "Lobbyist 25043 22 Ethnicity 13 Asian/GreaterEastAsian/EastAsian,... Party 3 Democrat, Independent, Republican Gender 6 Female, Male, Mostly Female, Mostly Male,..." (papers/text/Kim_2025__ai_bill_positions_lobbying.txt:2108-2124)
notes: Kim 2025-added enrichment (inferred features over LobbyView lobbyist records).

indicator_text: Client/interest group industry classification features used in Kim 2025 GNN (Industry, State, Government affiliation, Industry Type)
scoring_rule: N/A. One-hot encoded categorical features.
source_quote: Table 6 row "Interest Group 16093 557 Industry 426 ... State&Territories 59 NY, AL, ... + U.S. territories ... Government 2 True, False ... Industry Type 6 org, com, edu, net, gov, others" (papers/text/Kim_2025__ai_bill_positions_lobbying.txt:2016-2041)
notes: Kim 2025-added enrichment over the core client_uuid + primary_naics fields. 426-dimensional industry feature suggests a finer-grained taxonomy than NAICS alone.

indicator_text: Subject-matter classification of a bill (CRS subject term)
section_or_category: Bills table (Kim 2018) / Kim 2025 GNN node feature
scoring_rule: N/A. Many-to-many: a bill may have multiple subject terms.
source_quote: terms = ManyToMany('Term') (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:229-233); "looking at b.terms gives all of the terms that that bill is classified under" (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:230-231); Table 6 row "Bill 57466 114 Subject 34 Crime and law enforcement,,..." (papers/text/Kim_2025__ai_bill_positions_lobbying.txt:2045-2065)
notes: External-data field (CRS subject taxonomy). Not exposed as a first-class public-API field in the 2024 README BillResponse fields, but present in Kim 2018 schema and Kim 2025 GNN. Cross-ref items_Kim2018:bill_term.

indicator_text: CRS (Congressional Research Service) summary text of a bill
scoring_rule: N/A. Full-text indexed via Whoosh (2018) / Elasticsearch (production).
source_quote: we can get the date that the bill was introduced with b.introduced, get its CRS summary text with b.summary (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:206-207); "the CRS summary text of each bill, and the text of each lobbying report specific issue" (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:253-256)
notes: External-data field (CRS). Not exposed as a first-class field in the 2024 public-API BillResponse, but present in Kim 2018's database. Cross-ref items_Kim2018:bill_crs_summary.

indicator_text: Bill title — one bill may have multiple titles
scoring_rule: N/A. Used as the matching index for "bill mentioned by title only" detection.
source_quote: titles = OneToMany('BillTitle') (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:216); "Bills are sometimes only referred to by titles or alternate names. To account for this, we clean and tokenize the specific issue sections of the lobbying report, and perform a text matching operation against a table of bill titles" (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:84-87)
notes: External-data field. Cross-ref items_Kim2018:bill_title.

indicator_text: Firm-level financial features (employment, PPENT, COGS) merged from Compustat
scoring_rule: N/A. Compustat columns joined onto firms by name-disambiguated firm ID.
source_quote: Pulls firm-level financial and LDA report information from lobbying database (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:305-306); "Adds firm-level compustat financial data" (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:316); "net property, plant, and equipment (PPENT), and log of cost of goods sold (COGS). Both PPENT and COGS, along with the employment variable, are from the Compustat database" (papers/text/Kim_2025__ai_bill_positions_lobbying.txt:2422-2424)
notes: LobbyView-added enrichment (Compustat merge). Not exposed via public API; used in Kim 2018/2025 analyses. State-level: Compustat-equivalent enrichment is sparse for non-public-company state lobbying clients (non-profits, municipalities, ad-hoc coalitions). Cross-ref items_Kim2018:firm_financials.

indicator_text: Industry-level Herfindahl–Hirschman index — concentration of lobbying activity within an industry
scoring_rule: N/A. Computed per (industry, year). Industry grouping default = NAICS2.
source_quote: Herfindahl index measures the levels of competition among firms (clients) within the same industry (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:301-302); "Computes Herfindahl indices ... Outputs rows with firm information (sorted by industry as identified by NAICS2), industry Herfindahl index, and lobbying information for firm" (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:306-308)
notes: LobbyView-added derived metric. Not exposed via public API. Cross-ref items_Kim2018:industry_herfindahl.

indicator_text: REST API providing programmatic bulk download in standard formats — infrastructure rather than a data field
scoring_rule: N/A. PostgreSQL REST conventions; rate limits "100 requests per 24 hours ... 100 rows ... maximum 10,000 rows per 24 hours" per LobbyView API docs.
source_quote: we develop an API (Application Programming Interface) that enables researchers to bulk download the massive amount of unstructured data in standard formats so that they can conduct further analyses using preferred statistical software (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:14-17); "The limit of requests of each user is 100 requests per 24 hours" (lobbyview.org/data-download/api)
notes: LobbyView-added infrastructure. Cross-ref items_Kim2018:api_bulk_download.

indicator_text: Full-text search index over issue_text (LDA Section 16) and bill_summary (CRS) — Whoosh in 2018 draft, Elasticsearch in production
scoring_rule: N/A. Supports phrase and bag-of-words queries.
source_quote: Two types of data items are duplicated in a separate full-text index database to facilitate more efficient searching: the CRS summary text of each bill, and the text of each lobbying report specific issue (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:253-256); "the complex relational structure is stored using PostgreSQL and Elasticsearch" (papers/text/Kim_2018__lobbyview.txt:26-27)
notes: LobbyView-added infrastructure. Cross-ref items_Kim2018:full_text_index.
